items.title: Which PlayStation Plus Tier Is Actually Worth The Money?
items.desc: Are you a hardcore gamer or just a casual player? Your answer determines if a specific PS Plus tier is a waste of cash. Find out which plan fits your playstyle.

items.title: UAE QUITS OPEC weakening oil cartel's leverage over global prices
items.desc: As of May 1, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) will have lost one of its key members, weakening its leverage over global oil supplies and prices.

items.title: Live: King Charles to call for 'reconciliation and renewal' in address to US Congress
items.desc: King Charles is set to deliver a speech to Congress as part of his visit to the US to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence. Follow live.

items.title: The Big Idea: Marie Vibbert
items.desc: Though humans have a strong desire to be an individual, slightly stronger is our innate need to not be alone. Humans are not solitary creatures, so why do we try so hard to act like we are all just individuals with no ties or connections to those around us? A…

items.title: 142 guest teachers in Delhi may face termination for refusal to perform census duty
items.desc: Delhi may terminate 142 guest teachers for refusing census enumerator duty; GTA urges withdrawal, citing practical issues, low pay and seeking extra honorarium.

items.title: India fast-tracks Canada FTA, GCC talks paused, says Piyush Goyal
items.desc: India is pushing forward with trade deals. Negotiations for a free trade agreement with Canada are speeding up. Talks are also underway with Chile and Peru. However, discussions with the Gulf Cooperation Council have been temporarily halted due to the ongoing…

items.title: Saudi Aramco halts LPG exports through May after damage
items.desc: Saudi Aramco will halt liquefied petroleum gas shipments next month. The Juaymah export facility sustained damage in February. Repairs are delayed, preventing shipments through May. This disruption impacts buyers, especially in Asia. India, reliant on LPG for…

items.title: Melania Trump’s Python Heels Reappear for White House Ceremony
items.desc: Melania Trump wore white Manolo Blahnik for day two of the State Visit of King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Tuesday in Washington, D.C.

items.title: MCM set to hire agency for fresh waste processing
items.desc: MCM to hire private agency for 3-month fresh waste processing in Manesar at Rs 85 lakh; city generates 230–250 tonnes daily; weighbridge from May 1.

items.title: This Is Why We Need a Ballroom
items.desc: Many historical disasters would have been prevented if we had one of these in the White House.

items.title: 19-yr-old fatally stabbed in Narela; labourer beaten to death in Begumpur
items.desc: Two men killed in Delhi: 19-year-old stabbed in Narela Industrial Area; labourer beaten to death in Begumpur after argument. Police probe; one arrested.

items.title: India industrial output at 4.1%, hit by Iran war
items.desc: India's industrial output grew 4.1% in March, a five-month low. This slowdown was influenced by weaker electricity and manufacturing sectors. Economists noted the impact of the Iran conflict on supplies and costs. Despite the moderation, growth surpassed expe…

items.title: GM: Iran war causing cost increases, but pricey vehicles continue to sell
items.desc: GM CEO Mary Barra said the Detroit automaker continues to monitor any change in customer spending, but so far, the company's vehicle mix has remained healthy.

items.title: Trump tells King Charles his mother had a ‘crush’ on him during White House visit
items.desc: In a change of tone, Trump also praised the British military during Charles’ reception, saying that “nobody fought better together” with the US.

items.desc: DoI offers up to $885M if they abandon offshore wind projects As the Iran war pushes up energy prices, the Trump administration is paying offshore wind developers to walk away from projects and invest instead in fossil fuel infrastructure.…

items.title: A $500 million superyacht linked with Russia’s richest man was just able to pass through the blockaded Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: The Nord, a 466-foot vessel connected to Russian steel magnate Alexei Mordashov, is the 12th largest superyacht in the world.
